name: prompt-caching
description: Caching strategies for LLM prompts including Anthropic prompt caching, response caching, and CAG (Cache Augmented Generation) Use when: prompt caching, cache prompt, response cache, cag, cache augmented.
capabilities: Implement Anthropic native prompt caching for repeated prefixes
Design response caching strategies for identical or similar queries
Optimize prompts for maximum cache hit rates
Avoid caching anti-patterns like high-temperature responses
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a markdown documentation file with no executable code. The external_commands detections are markdown backticks used for code formatting. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are misinterpretations of YAML frontmatter content. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Reduce LLM API costs by up to 90% through strategic prompt caching implementation including Anthropic native caching, response caching, and CAG patterns.
userTitle: Optimize LLM Costs with Prompt Caching
limitations: Does not provide actual caching infrastructure code
Does not integrate with specific databases or caching systems
Temperature settings must be zero for reliable caching
Cache invalidation strategy must be designed by the user

promptTemplates.title: Basic Prompt Caching Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up prompt caching with Claude API? Show me the basic implementation steps.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Anthropic prompt caching

promptTemplates.title: Response Caching Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a response caching strategy for a Q&A system that handles similar user queries. Include cache key design and invalidation logic.

promptTemplates.title: CAG Implementation Guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain Cache Augmented Generation (CAG) and provide a Python implementation pattern for pre-caching documents.

promptTemplates.title: Cache Invalidation Best Practices
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the best practices for cache invalidation in LLM applications? Include time-based and event-based strategies.

bestPractices: Structure prompts with static prefixes that remain consistent across requests
Use zero temperature when caching responses for exact matches
Implement proper cache invalidation with time-based or event-based triggers
Monitor cache hit rates and optimize prefix structure accordingly
antiPatterns: Caching responses with high temperature settings - outputs will vary and cached data becomes useless
Caching without any invalidation strategy - stale data leads to incorrect responses
Caching everything indiscriminately - increases latency on cache misses without proportional benefit

faq.question: What is prompt caching?
faq.answer: Prompt caching is a technique that stores the computed state of a prompt prefix so it can be reused across multiple requests, reducing the number of tokens processed and lowering costs.

faq.question: How much can I save with prompt caching?
faq.answer: Users report cost reductions of 50-90% depending on how much of your prompts can be cached as stable prefixes.

faq.question: Does prompt caching work with all Claude models?
faq.answer: Prompt caching is supported by Claude models that support the cache_control parameter. Check the Anthropic API documentation for model compatibility.

faq.question: What is the difference between prompt caching and response caching?
faq.answer: Prompt caching uses the model's native ability to cache computed prefixes. Response caching is implemented by you - storing full responses for identical queries in your own storage.

faq.question: How long does the cache last?
faq.answer: Anthropic's prompt cache typically lasts 5 minutes, but this varies by API version. Response caching TTL is determined by your implementation.

faq.question: Can I cache responses with temperature greater than 0?
faq.answer: You should not cache responses with non-zero temperature because the outputs will vary, making cached data unreliable.
